data.name: App_Title
data.comment: The app title shown as the window title.

data.name: Shell_ProfileLabel
data.comment: Label next to the profile dropdown in the top bar.

data.name: Profile_DropdownPlaceholder
data.comment: Placeholder shown in the profile dropdown when nothing is selected.

data.name: ManageProfiles_ButtonTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the gear button that opens the Manage Profiles dialog.

data.name: Preferences_ButtonTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the button that opens the Preferences dialog.

data.name: Settings_ButtonTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the gear button that opens the Settings dialog (discovery paths + mod-repository location).

data.name: Launch_Button
data.comment: The Launch button in the shell top bar.

data.name: Status_GameRunning
data.comment: Status-strip label when Darktide is running.

data.name: Status_GameNotRunning
data.comment: Status-strip label when Darktide is not running.

data.name: Status_NxmRegistered
data.comment: Status-strip label when Curator is the registered OS handler for nxm:// links.

data.name: Status_NxmNotRegistered
data.comment: Status-strip label when Curator is not the registered OS handler for nxm:// links.

data.name: Status_NxmUnavailable
data.comment: Status-strip label when there is no platform registrar (not Windows or Linux).

data.name: Status_NxmRegisteredTooltip
data.value: Curator handles Nexus download links (nxm://). Manage this in Integrations.
data.comment: Tooltip on the NXM status-strip label when Curator is registered.

data.name: Status_NxmNotRegisteredTooltip
data.value: Curator is not set to handle Nexus download links (nxm://). Enable it in Integrations to download mods from Nexus.
data.comment: Tooltip on the NXM status-strip label when Curator is not registered. Does not assert a third-party handler; nothing may own nxm:// yet.

data.name: Status_NxmUnavailableTooltip
data.value: Curator cannot handle Nexus download links (nxm://) on this platform.
data.comment: Tooltip on the NXM status-strip label when there is no platform registrar.

data.name: ProfileSwitch_RunningTooltip
data.value: Darktide is running; stop it before switching profiles
data.comment: Tooltip on the profile dropdown explaining why switching is blocked.

data.name: ProfileSwitch_CreateFirstTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the profile dropdown when no profile exists.

data.name: AppUpdate_NoticeText
data.comment: Text on the dismissible update pill in the shell status strip. {0} is the available version.

data.name: AppUpdate_NoticeTooltip
data.value: A new Curator version is available. Click to update.
data.comment: Tooltip on the update pill in the shell status strip.

data.name: AppUpdate_DismissTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the dismiss button on the shell update pill (hides the notice for this session).

data.name: AppUpdate_ConfirmTitle
data.comment: Title of the confirm shown when the user clicks the shell update pill (download and restart).

data.name: AppUpdate_ConfirmMessage
data.value: Curator {0} is available. Download and restart now?
data.comment: Body of the update confirm. {0} is the available version.

data.name: AppUpdate_DownloadingTitle
data.comment: Title of the modal spinner shown while the update downloads.

data.name: AppUpdate_DownloadingMessage
data.value: Downloading the update. Curator will restart when ready.
data.comment: Body of the modal spinner shown while the update downloads.

data.name: AppUpdate_DownloadFailedTitle
data.comment: Title of the alert shown when the update download fails.

data.name: AppUpdate_DownloadFailedMessage
data.value: The update could not be downloaded.
data.comment: Prefix of the update download-failure alert body. The error detail follows.

data.name: AppUpdate_CheckFailedMessage
data.comment: Inline status shown in Settings when a manual update check fails.

data.name: ManageProfiles_Title
data.comment: Title of the Manage Profiles dialog.

data.name: ManageProfiles_NewButton
data.comment: The add-row button in the Manage Profiles dialog.

data.name: ManageProfiles_NewPlaceholder
data.comment: Placeholder in the inline add-row entry box.

data.name: ManageProfiles_RenameTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the pencil (rename) button on each profile row.

data.name: ManageProfiles_DeleteTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the trash (delete) button on each profile row.

data.name: ManageProfiles_DeleteLockedTooltip
data.value: Can't delete the active profile while Darktide is running.
data.comment: Tooltip on the trash button when it is disabled (active row held while Darktide runs).

data.name: ManageProfiles_DoneButton
data.comment: The Done button at the bottom of the Manage Profiles dialog.

data.name: ManageProfiles_DeleteTitle
data.comment: Title of the delete-profile confirmation prompt.

data.name: ManageProfiles_DeleteMessage
data.value: Delete profile {0}? This removes its mod list and staged launch root (the mod repository is untouched).
data.comment: Body of the delete-profile confirmation prompt. {0} is the profile name.

data.name: Dialog_CloseTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip + automation name on the drawn close (X) button in the shared modal title bar.

data.name: Confirm_Title
data.comment: Default title of the generic yes/no confirmation dialog.

data.name: Confirm_CancelButton
data.comment: The Cancel button in the confirm dialog.

data.name: Confirm_OkButton
data.comment: The OK / confirm button in the confirm dialog.

data.name: Preferences_Title
data.comment: Title of the Preferences dialog.

data.name: Preferences_ThemeHeader
data.comment: Header over the theme picker.

data.name: Preferences_ThemeSystem
data.comment: Theme picker option: follow the OS theme.

data.name: Preferences_ThemeDark
data.comment: Theme picker option: dark theme.

data.name: Preferences_ThemeLight
data.comment: Theme picker option: light theme.

data.name: Preferences_FontScaleHeader
data.comment: Header over the font-scale slider.

data.name: Preferences_LanguageHeader
data.comment: Header over the language dropdown.

data.name: Preferences_LanguageEnglish
data.comment: Language dropdown option: English (the only shipped language).

data.name: Preferences_DoneButton
data.comment: The Done button at the bottom of the Preferences dialog.

data.name: ModList_Header
data.comment: The mod-list header title (shown with or without an active profile; the row list itself is the count).

data.name: ModList_EmptyNoProfile
data.value: Select or create a profile to manage its mods.
data.comment: Empty-state message in the mod list when no profile is active.

data.name: ModList_EmptyNoMods
data.value: No mods yet. Add a mod (button or drag-and-drop) to get started.
data.comment: Empty-state message when the active profile has no mods.

data.name: ModList_AddButtonTooltip
data.value: Add a mod to this profile
data.comment: Tooltip on the Add split button in the mod list header (the primary click opens the current mode's picker).

data.name: ModList_AddArchive
data.comment: The archive-import label: the Add split button's primary label in archive mode + the archive flyout item. Curator auto-detects the archive format from the file contents via SharpCompress, so zip/7z/rar and other supported archives are all accepted.

data.name: ModList_AddFolder
data.comment: The folder-import label: the Add split button's primary label in folder mode + the folder flyout item.

data.name: ModList_DragDropHint
data.value: Drag mod files or folders onto the window to import (Windows only).
data.comment: Subtle hint in the no-mods empty state + the content-area tooltip, noting drag-and-drop is a Windows-only feature (XDND lands in Avalonia 12.1).

data.name: ModList_AutoSort
data.comment: Label next to the auto-sort checkbox in the mod list header.

data.name: ModList_AutoSortTooltip
data.value: Sort mods by their dependencies (dependency-based sorting is coming; currently no-op).
data.comment: Tooltip on the auto-sort checkbox. Notes that real sorting is coming; no em-dash.

data.name: ModRow_EnabledTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the enabled checkbox on each mod row.

data.name: ModRow_PolicyLatest
data.comment: Policy ComboBox option: track the newest release.

data.name: ModRow_PolicyPinned
data.comment: Policy ComboBox option: frozen at a specific release.

data.name: ModRow_PolicyPinnedDisplay
data.comment: Policy display text for a pinned mod (the badge / tooltip form). {0} is the pinned version tag.

data.name: ModRow_PinnedVersionPlaceholder
data.comment: Watermark / placeholder for the inline pinned-version editor.

data.name: ModRow_MoveUpTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the move-up button on each mod row.

data.name: ModRow_MoveDownTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the move-down button on each mod row.

data.name: ModRow_RemoveTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the trash (remove) button on each mod row.

data.name: ModRow_SourceUntracked
data.comment: Source badge text for a local / untracked mod.

data.name: ModRow_SourceNexus
data.comment: Source badge text for a Nexus Mods mod. {0} is the Nexus mod id.

data.name: ModRow_SourceGitHub
data.comment: Source badge text for a GitHub mod. {0} is the owner, {1} is the repo.

data.name: ModRow_NotFound
data.comment: Source badge text when a mod has no mod-store entry (stale profile reference).

data.name: ModRow_SourceBadgeLinkTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the source badge when it doubles as a link to the mod's remote page (Nexus mod page or GitHub repo).

data.name: ModRow_UpdateTooltipInstall
data.value: Update this mod to the latest release
data.comment: Tooltip on the stable update-action button for a Premium user when an update is available (click installs directly).

data.name: ModRow_UpdateTooltipOpenFiles
data.value: An update is available. Open the Nexus files page to download it
data.comment: Tooltip on the stable update-action button for a regular or unknown Premium user when an update is available (click opens the files page; Premium is required for a direct install).

data.name: ModRow_UpdateTooltipNoUpdate
data.comment: Tooltip on the stable update-action button when no update is available (the button is disabled).

data.name: ModList_RateLimited
data.comment: Short banner shown in the mod-list header when the last update check was rate-limited (the badges may not reflect every available update).

data.name: ModList_RateLimitedTooltip
data.value: The Nexus update check was rate-limited, so some available updates may not be shown. Try again later.
data.comment: Tooltip on the rate-limit banner in the mod-list header.

data.name: ModList_CheckNowTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the refresh button in the mod-list header; clicking it fires an immediate update check for the active profile.

data.name: ModList_ManualRefreshThrottled
data.value: Rate limiting protection enabled. Manual refresh will be available again in {0}.
data.comment: Tooltip on the refresh button while the manual sliding-window throttle is active. {0} is the remaining cooldown formatted as m:ss (e.g. 1:30). Replaces the normal Check for updates now tooltip until the cooldown elapses.

data.name: Update_FailedTitle
data.comment: Title of the modal alert shown when a per-mod one-click update fails.

data.name: Update_FailedMessage
data.comment: Prefix of the per-mod update-failure alert body. {0} is the mod's display name. The error detail follows.

data.name: ModList_OpenFilesFailedTitle
data.value: Could not open the files page
data.comment: Title of the alert shown when the regular-user update action (open the Nexus files page) fails to launch the browser.

data.name: ModList_OpenFilesFailedMessage
data.value: Could not open the Nexus files page for '{0}'. Visit it manually: {1}
data.comment: Body of the launcher-failure alert. {0} is the mod's display name; {1} is the files-page URL (for manual copy).

data.name: ModList_AutoUpdateFailedTitle
data.comment: Title of the aggregated alert shown when one or more automatic-update installs failed during a batch.

data.name: ModList_AutoUpdateFailedSummary
data.comment: Body of the aggregated automatic-update failure alert. {0} is the comma-separated list of failed mod names.

data.name: Import_Title
data.comment: Title of the per-mod import modal.

data.name: Import_NameLabel
data.comment: Label over the mod name field in the import modal.

data.name: Import_SourceLabel
data.comment: Label over the source chooser in the import modal.

data.name: Import_SourceUntracked
data.comment: Source chooser option: untracked / local.

data.name: Import_SourceNexus
data.comment: Source chooser option: Nexus Mods.

data.name: Import_SourceGitHub
data.comment: Source chooser option: GitHub.

data.name: Import_VersionLabel
data.comment: Label over the version field in the import modal.

data.name: Import_VersionRequired
data.comment: Validation message when the version field is empty for a remote source.

data.name: Import_NexusUrlLabel
data.comment: Label over the URL field when Nexus is chosen.

data.name: Import_GitHubUrlLabel
data.comment: Label over the URL field when GitHub is chosen.

data.name: Import_UrlPlaceholderNexus
data.comment: Watermark / placeholder for the Nexus URL field.

data.name: Import_UrlPlaceholderGitHub
data.comment: Watermark / placeholder for the GitHub repo URL field.

data.name: Import_OkButton
data.comment: The OK / confirm button in the import modal.

data.name: Import_CancelButton
data.comment: The Cancel button in the import modal.

data.name: Import_UrlRequired
data.value: A URL is required for this source.
data.comment: Validation message when the URL field is empty for a remote source.

data.name: Import_UrlInvalid
data.value: This URL does not look right. Check the format and try again.
data.comment: Validation message when the URL does not parse for the chosen source.

data.name: Import_PolicyLabel
data.comment: Label for the Latest / Pinned version-policy chooser in the import modal.

data.name: Import_PolicyLatest
data.comment: The Latest policy option in the import modal: track the mod's newest release.

data.name: Import_PolicyPinned
data.comment: The Pinned policy option in the import modal: freeze the mod at the version being imported.

data.name: RemoveMod_Title
data.comment: Title of the remove-mod confirmation prompt.

data.name: RemoveMod_Message
data.comment: Body of the remove-mod confirmation prompt. {0} is the mod name.

data.name: Import_FailedTitle
data.comment: Title of the modal alert shown when an import fails on an invalid source (bad archive/folder structure).

data.name: Import_FailedMessage
data.comment: Prefix of the import-failure alert body. {0} is the source path. The import's detail message follows.

data.name: Import_CollisionTitle
data.comment: Title of the modal alert shown when a mod's base folder name collides with an existing mod in the profile (the loader can't tell two same-folder mods apart).

data.name: Import_CollisionMessage
data.value: Could not import '{0}': its mod folder '{1}' is already used by '{2}' in this profile. Two mods with the same folder name can't coexist in one profile (the mod loader can't tell them apart).
data.comment: Body of the base-name collision alert. {0} is the source path; {1} is the conflicting base folder name; {2} is the existing mod's display name.

data.name: Settings_Title
data.comment: Title of the Settings dialog (discovery paths + mod-repository location).

data.name: Settings_DiscoverySection
data.comment: Header over the discovery-override section of the Settings dialog.

data.name: Settings_StorageSection
data.comment: Header over the storage section (mod-repository location) of the Settings dialog.

data.name: Settings_DoneButton
data.comment: The Done button at the bottom of the Settings dialog.

data.name: Settings_ModRepoLabel
data.comment: Label next to the mod-repository location TextBox in the Settings dialog.

data.name: Settings_UpdatesSection
data.comment: Header over the updates section of the Settings dialog (app self-update).

data.name: Settings_CurrentVersionLabel
data.comment: Label next to the installed Curator version in the Settings updates section.

data.name: Settings_VersionUnknown
data.comment: Shown in place of the version when it cannot be resolved (a non-packaged build).

data.name: Settings_CheckForUpdatesButton
data.comment: The manual update-check button in the Settings updates section.

data.name: Settings_DownloadAndRestartButton
data.comment: The download-and-restart button in the Settings updates section, shown when an update is available.

data.name: Settings_UpToDate
data.comment: Inline status shown in Settings after a manual check finds no update.

data.name: Settings_UpdateAvailable
data.comment: Inline status shown in Settings when an update is available. {0} is the available version.

data.name: Settings_CheckOnStartup
data.value: Check for Curator updates on startup
data.comment: Label next to the startup update-check toggle in the Settings updates section.

data.name: Settings_CheckOnStartupTooltip
data.value: When off, Curator will not check for new versions on startup. You can still check manually.
data.comment: Tooltip on the startup update-check toggle in the Settings updates section.

data.name: Discovery_SteamInstallLabel
data.comment: Label for the Steam install directory field (folder picker).

data.name: Discovery_DarktideBinaryLabel
data.comment: Label for the Darktide.exe path field (file picker).

data.name: Discovery_CompatdataLabel
data.comment: Label for the Wine prefix / compatdata directory field (folder picker, Linux only).

data.name: Discovery_ProtonBinaryLabel
data.comment: Label for the proton script path field (file picker, Linux only).

data.name: Discovery_BrowseButton
data.comment: The Browse button next to every discovery / ModsFolder row.

data.name: EscapeHatch_Title
data.comment: Title of the discovery escape-hatch dialog shown on a DiscoveryIncomplete launch.

data.name: EscapeHatch_Header
data.value: We couldn't automatically discover everything needed to launch. Please help configure these paths.
data.comment: The friendly header of the escape-hatch dialog (NOT framed as an error).

data.name: EscapeHatch_SubmitButton
data.comment: The Submit button on the escape-hatch dialog (writes the entered paths + closes).

data.name: EscapeHatch_CancelButton
data.comment: The Cancel button on the escape-hatch dialog.

data.name: EscapeHatch_RetryHint
data.value: Click Launch to retry after updating.
data.comment: Note on the escape-hatch dialog telling the user to click Launch again after submitting (no auto-retry).

data.name: Launch_LaunchedNote
data.comment: The transient status-strip note after a successful launch. {0} is the profile name.

data.name: Launch_ErrorTitle
data.comment: Title of the modal alert shown when a launch returns LaunchStatus.Error.

data.name: Launch_StagingFailedTitle
data.comment: Title of the modal alert shown when a launch returns LaunchStatus.StagingFailed (a staging link could not be created).

data.name: Launch_StagingFailedMessage
data.value: Curator couldn't create the staging links for this profile. Check that Curator has write access to the profile's folder, and on Windows that the folder is on an NTFS volume (staging links require NTFS).
data.comment: Localized framing + actionable hint for the StagingFailed alert body. The raised exception's body (a runtime/OS error) is appended after this at the call site, so this reads as a lead-in to the detail.

data.name: Integrations_ButtonTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the shell button that opens the Integrations dialog.

data.name: Integrations_Title
data.comment: Title of the Integrations dialog.

data.name: Integrations_NexusHeader
data.comment: Header over the Nexus section of the Integrations dialog.

data.name: Integrations_LoginWithNexus
data.comment: The primary OAuth login button in the Integrations dialog.

data.name: Integrations_LoginWithNexusTooltip
data.value: Sign in via Nexus Mods (opens your browser to consent).
data.comment: Tooltip on the Log in with Nexus button.

data.name: Integrations_ApiKeyLabel
data.comment: Label over the API-key TextBox in the Integrations dialog.

data.name: Integrations_ApiKeyWatermark
data.comment: Watermark in the API-key TextBox.

data.name: Integrations_ValidateButton
data.comment: The Validate button next to the API-key TextBox.

data.name: Integrations_ApiKeyHelp
data.comment: The "Get an API key" link under the API-key field (opens the Nexus API-keys page).

data.name: Integrations_ApiKeyHelpUrl
data.comment: The Nexus API-keys page URL. Used as the link target + the help-link tooltip.

data.name: Integrations_ApiKeyTestingNote
data.value: For testing only. This option is removed at release.
data.comment: Small, low-opacity note inside the API-key block signaling its pre-registration status. The release-time remove-vs-hide decision is separate.

data.name: Integrations_SignOutButton
data.comment: The Sign out button (shown when authenticated).

data.name: Integrations_DoneButton
data.comment: The Done button at the bottom of the Integrations dialog.

data.name: Integrations_StatusNotSignedIn
data.comment: Status line when no Nexus auth method is configured.

data.name: Integrations_StatusSignedInOAuth
data.value: Signed in as {0} (Regular) via Nexus login.
data.comment: Status line when signed in via OAuth (non-premium). {0} is the user's display name.

data.name: Integrations_StatusSignedInOAuthPremium
data.value: Signed in as {0} (Premium) via Nexus login.
data.comment: Status line when signed in via OAuth with a premium account. {0} is the user's display name.

data.name: Integrations_StatusSignedInOAuthUnverified
data.value: Signed in via Nexus login (couldn't verify the account; check your network).
data.comment: Status line when OAuth is configured but the verify call failed.

data.name: Integrations_StatusSignedInApiKey
data.value: Signed in as {0} (Regular) via API key.
data.comment: Status line when signed in via API key (non-premium). {0} is the user's display name.

data.name: Integrations_StatusSignedInApiKeyPremium
data.value: Signed in as {0} (Premium) via API key.
data.comment: Status line when signed in via API key with a premium account. {0} is the user's display name.

data.name: Integrations_StatusSignedInApiKeyUnverified
data.value: Signed in via API key (couldn't verify the account; check your network).
data.comment: Status line when an API key is configured but the verify call failed.

data.name: Integrations_StatusSignedInGeneric
data.comment: Fallback status line for an unexpected auth-state shape. Not expected in practice (None is null; OAuth + ApiKey are the only non-None values).

data.name: Integrations_ShowApiKeyTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the eye-toggle button in the masked state (click to reveal).

data.name: Integrations_HideApiKeyTooltip
data.comment: Tooltip on the eye-toggle button in the revealed state (click to mask).

data.name: Integrations_ErrorFormat
data.comment: Inline status format for an auth failure (carries the error message). {0} is the error message.

data.name: Integrations_StartingOAuth
data.value: Opening your browser to sign in…
data.comment: Status line shown while the OAuth flow is starting (before the browser opens).

data.name: Integrations_Validating
data.comment: Status line shown while the API-key Validate call is in flight.

data.name: Integrations_ApiKeyEmpty
data.comment: Status line shown when the user clicks Validate with an empty API-key TextBox.

data.name: Integrations_AutoUpdateHeader
data.comment: Header over the update-check settings sub-section in the Integrations dialog.

data.name: Integrations_AutoUpdateEnabled
data.comment: Label next to the periodic update-check toggle in the Integrations dialog.

data.name: Integrations_AutoUpdateInterval
data.comment: Label next to the periodic update-check interval input in the Integrations dialog.

data.name: Integrations_AutomaticUpdates
data.comment: Label next to the opt-in automatic mod-update installation toggle in the Integrations dialog. Premium-only.

data.name: Integrations_AutomaticUpdatesTooltip
data.value: When on, flagged Nexus mod updates are installed automatically after a check runs. Only verified Premium accounts can enable this.
data.comment: Tooltip on the automatic-updates toggle when the account is verified Premium.

data.name: Integrations_AutomaticUpdatesPremiumRequired
data.value: Automatic installation requires a verified Premium Nexus account.
data.comment: Tooltip on the automatic-updates toggle when the account is regular or unverified (shown via ShowOnDisabled while the checkbox is disabled).

data.name: Integrations_NxmHeader
data.comment: Header over the nxm:// handler section of the Integrations dialog.

data.name: Integrations_NxmStatusRegistered
data.comment: Status line in the nxm section when Curator is the registered OS handler.

data.name: Integrations_NxmStatusNotRegistered
data.value: Curator is not set to handle Nexus download links (nxm://).
data.comment: Status line in the nxm section when Curator is not the registered OS handler. Does not assert a third-party handler; nothing may own nxm:// yet.

data.name: Integrations_NxmStatusUnavailable
data.comment: Status line in the nxm section when there is no platform registrar.

data.name: Integrations_NxmRegisterLabel
data.comment: The toggle button label when Curator is not registered (click to register).

data.name: Integrations_NxmUnregisterLabel
data.comment: The toggle button label when Curator is registered (click to unregister, releasing the handler so other managers can use it).

data.name: Integrations_NxmActionTooltipRegistered
data.value: Stop Curator from handling nxm:// links (releases the handler so other mod managers can use it).
data.comment: Tooltip on the toggle button when Curator is registered.

data.name: Integrations_NxmActionTooltipNotRegistered
data.value: Make Curator the handler for nxm:// Nexus download links.
data.comment: Tooltip on the toggle button when Curator is not registered.

data.name: Integrations_NxmActionTooltipUnavailable
data.value: Curator cannot register as the nxm:// handler on this platform.
data.comment: Tooltip on the toggle button when there is no platform registrar.

data.name: Integrations_NxmConfirmTitle
data.comment: Title of the confirmation prompt shown before registering Curator as the nxm:// handler.

data.name: Integrations_NxmConfirmMessage
data.value: This changes the system-wide handler for Nexus download links (nxm://). It may affect Vortex, Mod Organizer 2, Nexus Mod Manager, or other mod managers. Curator only supports Darktide downloads. Continue?
data.comment: Body of the nxm registration confirmation prompt. Warns it is a system-wide change that can affect other mod managers.

data.name: Integrations_NxmRegisterFailedTitle
data.comment: Title of the alert shown when registering the nxm:// handler fails.

data.name: Integrations_NxmRegisterFailedMessage
data.comment: Body of the nxm register-failure alert. {0} is the error message.

data.name: Integrations_NxmUnregisterFailedTitle
data.value: Could not release the nxm handler
data.comment: Title of the alert shown when unregistering the nxm:// handler fails.

data.name: Integrations_NxmUnregisterFailedMessage
data.comment: Body of the nxm unregister-failure alert. {0} is the error message.

data.name: Dmf_AddTitle
data.comment: Title of the confirm shown when DMF is already in the repository but not in the active profile (case 1: instant add, no download).

data.name: Dmf_AddMessage
data.value: DMF (Darktide Mod Framework) is installed but not in this profile. Add it?
data.comment: Body of the case-1 confirm. DMF is required for most Darktide mods.

data.name: Dmf_DownloadTitle
data.comment: Title of the confirm shown when DMF is not in the repository (case 2: download + add, or browser-open for non-premium users).

data.name: Dmf_DownloadMessage
data.value: DMF (Darktide Mod Framework) is required for most Darktide mods. Download and add it to this profile? On the next screen, click Download on the DMF Nexus page.
data.comment: Body of the case-2 confirm when Curator owns the nxm:// handler (the manager-download path: the user clicks Download on Nexus and the handler picks it up).

data.name: Dmf_DownloadMessageManual
data.value: DMF (Darktide Mod Framework) is required for most Darktide mods. Download and add it to this profile? On the next screen, download the DMF archive, then import it into Curator.
data.comment: Body of the case-2 confirm when Curator is not the nxm:// handler (manual-import guidance: the user downloads the archive and imports it via the normal add flow).

data.name: Dmf_Downloading
data.comment: Title of the alert shown while the DMF download is in flight (a modal spinner). No em-dash.

data.name: Dmf_DownloadingMessage
data.value: Downloading DMF from Nexus Mods. This takes a few seconds.
data.comment: Body of the in-flight DMF download alert.

data.name: Dmf_DownloadFailedTitle
data.comment: Title of the alert shown when the DMF download fails.

data.name: Dmf_DownloadFailedMessage
data.comment: Body of the DMF download-failure alert. {0} is the error message. The user can retry via the normal add flow.

data.name: Dmf_OpenBrowserFailedMessage
data.value: Could not open the DMF page in your browser. Visit this URL to download DMF: {0}
data.comment: Body of the alert shown when the OS shell-open of the DMF files URL fails (no default browser, headless, etc.). {0} is the DMF files URL. The user can copy it manually.

data.name: Nxm_NonDarktideTitle
data.comment: Title of the alert shown when an nxm:// download link is for a game other than Darktide.

data.name: Nxm_NonDarktideMessage
data.value: Curator only handles Darktide Nexus downloads. This link is for {0}.
data.comment: Body of the non-Darktide alert. {0} is the Nexus game domain from the link.

data.name: Nxm_NotConfiguredTitle
data.comment: Title of the alert shown when an nxm:// download arrives but Nexus auth is not configured.

data.name: Nxm_NotConfiguredMessage
data.value: Configure the Nexus integration (OAuth or API key) before downloading mods.
data.comment: Body of the not-configured alert.

data.name: Nxm_NoActiveProfileTitle
data.comment: Title of the alert shown when an nxm:// download arrives but no profile is active.

data.name: Nxm_NoActiveProfileMessage
data.value: Select or create a profile before downloading mods.
data.comment: Body of the no-active-profile alert.

data.name: Nxm_DownloadFailedTitle
data.comment: Title of the alert shown when an nxm:// download or its profile registration fails.

data.name: Welcome_Title
data.comment: Title of the first-run Welcome window.

data.name: Welcome_Intro
data.value: Curator organizes your Darktide mods into separate profiles and keeps them up to date.
data.comment: Intro paragraph in the Welcome window. Concise user-facing prose.

data.name: Welcome_CapabilityProfiles
data.comment: Bullet: profile-based mod management capability.

data.name: Welcome_CapabilityUpdates
data.value: Check your installed mods for updates
data.comment: Bullet: Nexus update-check capability.

data.name: Welcome_CapabilityNxm
data.value: Use Nexus "Download with manager" links directly
data.comment: Bullet: nxm download-link handling capability.

data.name: Welcome_SetUpNexus
data.comment: Accent primary button label. Opens the Integrations dialog after Welcome closes.

data.name: Welcome_Continue
data.comment: Secondary button label. Skips Nexus setup; leaves the user at the main window.
